rationale.R1.2: A general review of a research-paper section centrally includes checking whether the argument from benchmark limitations to RoboJudge results and recommendation is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes empirical claims from reported correlations and comparisons, so review centrally includes checking whether those claims are supported by the stated results.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation asks to review a section rather than redesign the whole paper, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and polish are expected responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer should flag uncertainty where the section does not provide enough information to verify a point.
rationale.RX.2: The strong recommendation to replace predicate-based metrics makes overclaim avoidance an active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within reviewing the provided section and not execute edits or broader paper assessment.
rationale.RX.4: The review should consider whether claims could mislead downstream benchmark practice or evaluation use.
rationale.RX.5: Any review judgments should be traceable to specific statements or evidence in the provided artifact.
